title: Photos: Boston kicks off Fourth of July celebration
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: Boston's July 4th celebration began with a parade and Declaration of Independence reading at the Old State House.
Crowds attended festivities including confetti-filled parades and events on the Esplanade for the nation's 250th anniversary.
Mayor Michelle Wu participated, with historical groups and the Boston Crusaders featured in the parade.

title: Is Caleb Durbin’s turnaround enough to solidify his future with the Red Sox?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Caleb Durbin leads the Red Sox in OPS (.977) and home runs with seven over the past 30 days.
Durbin improved his performance after working with a new personal hitting coach mid-season in 2026.
Red Sox control Durbin's contract until 2031, offering flexibility in roster decisions and payroll management.

title: Red Sox’ Aroldis Chapman breaks MLB relief pitcher strikeout record in save vs. Angels
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox closer Aroldis Chapman set the MLB record for most strikeouts by a relief pitcher with 1,364.
Chapman achieved the milestone in Boston's 5-2 win against the Angels, surpassing Hoyt Wilhelm's previous record.
Chapman, who joined the Red Sox in 2025, thanked the team for celebrating his accomplishment with a video montage.

title: Allstate names the most collision-prone cities in the U.S.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston named most collision-prone U.S. city for second year; drivers average 3.7 years between collisions.
Worcester and Springfield, MA, are among the bottom five for collision frequency, ranking 197th and 196th respectively.
Providence, RI, ranks 194th in collision frequency, with average collisions every 5.87 years, per Allstate's report.

title: Falmouth man, 74, arrested after police say he led troopers on wrong-way, high-speed chase over Bourne Bridge
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Armando Deleon, 74, was arrested after leading police on a 105 mph wrong-way chase over the Bourne Bridge.
The pursuit involved assault with a dangerous weapon and ended when officers removed Deleon from his barricaded home.
Deleon faces over a dozen charges and is held without bail, scheduled to return to court on August 5.

title: Boston police release new photos of alleged lemonade stand robber
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston police released photos of a second juvenile suspect in the lemonade stand armed robbery on West Ninth Street.
The robbery involved two males who stole a cash box after allegedly displaying a firearm on June 10.
Officials seek public assistance in identifying the second suspect, offering anonymous tip lines for information.

title: Jayson Tatum and Brad Stevens appear on Rich Paul’s big board of LeBron James landing spots
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: LeBron James is considering new NBA teams for his final season after leaving the Lakers.
Boston Celtics' Brad Stevens and Jayson Tatum appear on a speculative list of James's possible new teams.
Top contenders for James include Philadelphia, Miami, Denver, and Minnesota, with Boston less likely.

title: Robert Parish not a fan of Celtics’ decision to trade Jaylen Brown: ‘If Red [Auerbach] was alive … I think this would have made him nauseated’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Robert Parish criticized the Celtics' decision to trade Jaylen Brown to the Philadelphia 76ers.
Parish compared the trade to the Mavericks' controversial decision to trade Luka Doncic.
Parish speculated that Red Auerbach would be displeased by Brown's trade within the division.

title: Jayson Tatum bids farewell to Jaylen Brown: ‘Nothing but love and respect for you’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jayson Tatum expressed gratitude towards Jaylen Brown in a farewell message on social media after Brown's trade.
Jaylen Brown was traded to the Philadelphia 76ers for Paul George and draft picks, surprising Brown.
Brown and Tatum played nine seasons together, winning the 2024 NBA Finals with Brown named Finals MVP.

title: Celtics, Neemias Queta reportedly agree to four-year extension
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Celtics extend Neemias Queta's contract for four more years, totaling $56 million, through the 2030-31 season.
Queta's 2026-27 team option picked up; originally drafted by Kings in 2021, joined Celtics in 2023 on a two-way contract.
Queta averaged 10.2 points, 8.4 rebounds, 1.3 blocks in 2025-26, placing fourth in Most Improved Player voting.
